title: Data Dictionary for NYC_311_SR_2010-2020-sample-1M.csv
description:
**Description**

The dataset comprises 1 000 000 unique New York City 311 complaint records collected between January 2010 and December 2020. Each record is identified by a system‑generated integer key and contains a rich set of attributes describing the incident, including agency responsible, complaint category and descriptor, location details (address, ZIP code, borough, latitude/longitude), status, timestamps for creation, closure, due dates, and resolution narrative. The data also records additional contextual information such as community board designation, vehicle type, taxi pickup description, bridge/highway identifiers, and park or recreation facility involvement. Across the dataset, 68 8837 complaints have been formally closed while a small proportion remain open or pending. The geographic coordinates span the five NYC boroughs, with latitude values between 40.11°N and 40.91°N and longitude values between –77.52°W and –73.70°W.

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Central tendency & spread**: Created dates cluster around the mid‑2010s (median ≈ 2015‑11) with a range of ~4009 days (~11 years). Closed dates exhibit a similar spread, while due dates are highly skewed toward the early 2010s and include a large “Other” bucket covering 99.6% of records.
- **Distribution shape**: Many categorical fields (e.g., Complaint Type, Descriptor, Location Type) display extreme right‑skewness with a few frequent values (e.g., “Noise – Residential”, “Loud Music/Party”) followed by a large “Other” category that captures the remaining 50–70 % of observations. Numeric coordinates are broadly distributed across New York City but contain a few outliers at the extreme ends of the state‑plane coordinate range.
- **Missing values**: Several fields have substantial null counts—Incident Zip (54 978), Incident Address (174 700), City (61 963), and Vehicle Type (999 652). These gaps suggest incomplete address or vehicle reporting in many complaints.
- **High cardinality & sparsity**: Fields such as BBL, X/Y coordinates, Latitude/Longitude, and the detailed location strings possess very high cardinality with most values appearing only once, leading to sparse data matrices for any analytical modeling that relies on these attributes.
- **Duplicate safety**: The `Unique Key` field guarantees record uniqueness; no duplicate rows exist in the dataset.
- **Potential outliers & placeholder values**: Closed dates occasionally take unrealistic future values (e.g., 2100‑01‑01) likely used as placeholders for missing closure information. Latitude/Longitude pairs that are identical to known public coordinates may inadvertently reveal sensitive location details, raising privacy considerations.
- **Data quality issues**: Inconsistent address formatting (extra spaces, mixed case), mixed usage of ZIP codes (“XXXXX” placeholder), and the presence of a large “Other (N)” bucket in frequency distributions obscure fine‑grained analysis. The dataset also contains textual fields with long narratives that may contain sensitive personal information; users should apply de‑identification or filtering if required for compliance.

*Attribution: Generated by qsv v20.1.0 describegpt

Unique Key.title: Record ID
Unique Key.description: A system‑generated numeric identifier that uniquely identifies each complaint record in the dataset.

Created Date.title: Creation Timestamp
Created Date.description: The date and time when the complaint was first entered into the system. This timestamp marks the beginning of the record’s lifecycle.

Closed Date.title: Closure Timestamp
Closed Date.description: The date and time when the complaint was closed or finalized. May be null for complaints that remain open.

Agency.title: Agency Code
Agency.description: An abbreviated code identifying the city agency responsible for handling the complaint (e.g., NYPD, DOT).

Agency Name.title: Agency Full Name
Agency Name.description: The full name of the agency that addressed the complaint.

Incident Zip.title: Incident ZIP Code
Incident Zip.description: The five‑digit ZIP code in which the complaint was reported.

Incident Address.title: Incident Street Address
Incident Address.description: A street address or intersection where the incident took place, including building number and primary street name. This field is the main component of the mailing address for the incident location.

Street Name.description: The name of the primary street involved in the complaint location; combine with Incident Address, Cross Streets, or Intersection Streets to form a complete address.

Cross Street 1.title: Cross Street 1
Cross Street 1.description: The first cross street or intersection point adjacent to the incident location, if applicable. It is part of the detailed location context.

Cross Street 2.title: Cross Street 2
Cross Street 2.description: A second cross street near the incident location, when available.

Intersection Street 1.title: Intersection Street 1
Intersection Street 1.description: The first street involved in an intersection where the complaint occurred; part of the intersection definition.

Intersection Street 2.title: Intersection Street 2
Intersection Street 2.description: The second street involved in an intersection where the complaint occurred.

Address Type.title: Address Representation Type
Address Type.description: A categorical indicator of how the address is represented (e.g., ADDRESS, INTERSECTION, BLOCKFACE, LATLONG).

City.title: Incident City / Borough
City.description: The city or borough within New York City where the incident was reported.

Landmark.title: Nearby Landmark
Landmark.description: A notable landmark or street name that helps identify the location of the complaint.

Facility Type.title: Facility Category
Facility Type.description: The type of facility involved in the incident (e.g., DSNY Garage, School District).

Status.description: The current status of the complaint record (e.g., Closed, Pending, Open).

Resolution Description.description: A narrative summary of actions taken to resolve the complaint, including findings or outcomes.

Resolution Action Updated Date.title: Last Resolution Update Timestamp
Resolution Action Updated Date.description: The most recent datetime when the resolution action was updated or recorded.

Community Board.title: Community Board Designation
Community Board.description: The community board number or designation responsible for the area where the incident occurred.

BBL.title: Borough Block Lot (BBL)
BBL.description: A ten‑digit numeric identifier used by NYC’s Department of Finance to reference a specific borough block lot.

Borough.title: Incident Borough
Borough.description: The New York City borough in which the incident took place (e.g., Brooklyn, Queens).

X Coordinate (State Plane).title: State Plane X Coordinate
X Coordinate (State Plane).description: The easting coordinate of the incident location expressed in the New York State Plane coordinate system.

Y Coordinate (State Plane).title: State Plane Y Coordinate
Y Coordinate (State Plane).description: The northing coordinate of the incident location expressed in the New York State Plane coordinate system.

Open Data Channel Type.title: Submission Medium
Open Data Channel Type.description: The medium through which the complaint was originally submitted (e.g., PHONE, ONLINE).

Park Facility Name.title: Park or Recreation Facility Name
Park Facility Name.description: The name of a park or recreational facility involved in the incident, if applicable.

Taxi Pick Up Location.description: A textual description of the location where a taxi was requested to pick up a passenger.

Latitude.title: Latitude (Decimal Degrees)
Latitude.description: The geographic latitude coordinate of the incident location, expressed in decimal degrees. Combine with Longitude to form a full coordinate pair.

Longitude.title: Longitude (Decimal Degrees)
Longitude.description: The geographic longitude coordinate of the incident location, expressed in decimal degrees. Combine with Latitude to form a full coordinate pair.

Location.title: Coordinate String
Location.description: A textual representation of the incident's geographic coordinates formatted as (latitude, longitude). This field is an alternative string form of Latitude and Longitude.
